prTitle: fix(workflow): gate worktree-root resolution on isWorktree (closes #114)
prBody:
## Summary

Ports gsd-pi commit \`49d3fc9\` ("fix(gsd): resolve worktree registry root from checkout") to otto-cli as an **adapted-port**.

otto already had the \`resolveGitWorkingTreeRoot\` helper (landed in the broad hard-fork commit) but invoked it **unconditionally** in \`createRepositoryRegistry\`, running a synchronous \`git rev-parse --show-toplevel\` on every registry build. For a **non-worktree** bootstrapped project root nested inside an enclosing git repo, this incorrectly redirected \`projectRoot\` to the enclosing git toplevel.

This change gates the lookup behind \`contract.isWorktree\` (matching upstream intent, adapted to otto's \`workflow\` paths and \`resolveWorkflowPathContract\`):

\`\`\`ts
const projectRoot = contract.isWorktree
  ? resolveGitWorkingTreeRoot(contract.workRoot) ?? contract.projectRoot
  : contract.projectRoot;
\`\`\`

\`isWorktree\` is an existing field on \`WorkflowPathContract\` — no extra export needed.

## Gates

- **Regression test** — added to \`tests/repository-registry.test.ts\`, pinning that a non-worktree root nested in a git repo is NOT redirected to the enclosing toplevel. Verified **fails before / passes after** the gate.
- **Build** — pass.
- **Targeted suite** — pass.
- **Independent reviewer** — approve (gate faithfully adapts upstream; \`isWorktree\` is a real field; test is non-tautological; commit touches only the two target files).
- **Full suite** — green (an unrelated flaky cmux stdio-isolation test failed on the first run, passed 14/14 in isolation and on full re-run; out of scope of this fix).

Resolved: 1 / Unresolved: 0

Closes #114.
